info.name: EasyRisparmio - Authentication
info.description:
Authentication API collection for the EasyRisparmio Italian energy comparison & switching platform.

## Authentication Methods
- **Email/Password** — local registration and login with bcrypt-hashed passwords
- **Social Login** — Google, Facebook, Apple via Firebase ID token verification

## Roles
| Role | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `personal` | Individual user (default) |
| `business` | Business user with company profile |
| `admin` | Platform administrator (seeded, cannot register) |

## Token Flow
1. Login or social-login returns `accessToken` (JWT, 15 min) + `refreshToken` (UUID, 7 days)
2. Use `accessToken` in `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header for protected endpoints
3. When expired, call refresh-token to get a new pair

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your server (default: `http://localhost:3000`)
2. Register a user, then login — tokens are auto-saved to collection variables
3. Protected requests (`Get Profile`) automatically use the saved `accessToken`

variable.description: Base URL of the EasyRisparmio API server
JWT access token (auto-set by login/social-login requests)
UUID refresh token (auto-set by login/social-login requests)

item.name: Registration
item.description: User registration endpoints. After registration, the account status is `pending_verification` until the email OTP is verified. Admin role cannot be used.

request.description:
Creates a personal user account.

After registration, a 6-digit OTP is generated for email verification. The account status is `pending_verification` until the OTP is verified via `POST /auth/verify-otp`.

**Validation Rules:**
- `email` — must be a valid email, unique across all users
- `password` — minimum 8 characters
- `firstName` / `lastName` — required, max 100 characters
- `phone` — optional, max 20 characters
- `role` — must be `personal` or `business` (admin is blocked)

response.name: 201 - Registration Successful
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "message": "Registration successful. Please verify your email.",
    "user": {
      "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
      "email": "mario.rossi@email.com",
      "firstName": "Mario",
      "lastName": "Rossi",
      "phone": "+393331234567",
      "role": "personal",
      "status": "pending_verification",
      "authProvider": "local",
      "firebaseUid": null,
      "emailVerified": false,
      "phoneVerified": false,
      "createdAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}

response.name: 400 - Validation Error

request.description:
Creates a business user account with a company profile.

Extends personal registration with Italian business fields.

**Additional Fields:**
- `companyName` — required, max 255 characters
- `partitaIva` — required, exactly 11 digits (Italian VAT number)
- `pecEmail` — optional, valid email (certified PEC email)
- `legalRepresentative` — optional, max 255 characters
- `companyType` — optional (e.g. S.r.l., S.p.A.)
- `atecoCode` — optional, max 10 characters (economic activity code)

response.name: 201 - Business Registration Successful
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "message": "Registration successful. Please verify your email.",
    "user": {
      "id": "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-bcde-f12345678901",
      "email": "info@rossi-srl.it",
      "firstName": "Mario",
      "lastName": "Rossi",
      "phone": "+393331234567",
      "role": "business",
      "status": "pending_verification",
      "authProvider": "local",
      "firebaseUid": null,
      "emailVerified": false,
      "phoneVerified": false,
      "createdAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T10:00:00.000Z"
    }
  }
}

response.name: 400 - Invalid Partita IVA

item.name: Login
item.description: Authentication endpoints for email/password and social login. Both return a JWT access token and UUID refresh token on success.

request.description:
Authenticates with email and password.

Returns a JWT access token (15 min expiry) and a UUID refresh token (7 days expiry).

**Login is blocked when:**
- Email or password is incorrect
- Account status is `pending_verification` (email not yet verified)
- Account status is `suspended`
- User is social-only (no password set) — returns generic invalid credentials

**Post-request script** automatically saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.

response.name: 401 - Email Not Verified

request.description:
Login or register via Google, Facebook, or Apple using Firebase Authentication.

**Flow:**
1. Mobile app initiates social sign-in via Firebase Auth SDK
2. Firebase returns an ID token to the mobile app
3. Mobile app sends the ID token to this endpoint
4. Server verifies the token with `firebase-admin` SDK
5. Server extracts user info (email, name, avatar, provider)

**Behavior:**
- **New user** — account created with `role: personal`, `status: active`, `emailVerified: true`, `passwordHash: null`
- **Existing email** — Firebase UID linked to existing account; user can still use email/password login
- **Returning social user** — looked up by Firebase UID, logged in directly

**Provider detection** is automatic from the decoded token:
- `google.com` → `google`
- `facebook.com` → `facebook`
- `apple.com` → `apple`

**Post-request script** automatically saves tokens to collection variables.

response.name: 400 - Social Account Has No Email
400 - Invalid Firebase ID Token

item.name: OTP Verification
item.description: OTP (One-Time Password) verification for email confirmation, phone verification, and password reset flows. Codes are 6 digits, valid for 10 minutes, and single-use.

request.description:
Verifies a 6-digit OTP code.

**OTP Types:**
- `email_verification` — activates the account (`emailVerified: true`, `status: active`)
- `phone_verification` — marks phone as verified (`phoneVerified: true`)
- `password_reset` — validates reset code (used by reset-password endpoint separately)

**OTP Behavior:**
- 6-digit numeric code
- Expires after 10 minutes
- Single-use (marked as used after successful verification)
- Generating a new OTP invalidates any previous unused OTPs of the same type

item.name: Password Reset
item.description:
Two-step password reset flow:
1. Request a reset OTP via `forgot-password` (always returns the same response to prevent user enumeration)
2. Submit the OTP + new password via `reset-password`

After a successful reset, all existing refresh tokens are revoked for security.

request.description:
Requests a password reset OTP to be sent to the user's email.

**Security:** Always returns the same response regardless of whether the email exists in the system. This prevents user enumeration attacks.

The generated OTP is valid for 10 minutes. Any previous unused password-reset OTPs for this user are invalidated.

response.name: 200 - Reset OTP Sent (or email not found — same response)

request.description:
Resets the user's password using a valid OTP code from the `forgot-password` endpoint.

**Fields:**
- `email` — the user's registered email
- `code` — 6-digit OTP from the forgot-password email
- `newPassword` — new password, minimum 8 characters

**Security:** After a successful password reset, all existing refresh tokens for the user are revoked. The user must log in again with the new password.

item.name: Token Management
item.description:
Access token refresh and authenticated profile retrieval.

**Token lifecycle:**
- Access token (JWT): 15 minutes, sent via `Authorization: Bearer` header
- Refresh token (UUID): 7 days, stored in database, single-use with rotation

exec: console.log('New tokens saved to collection variables');

request.description:
Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token + refresh token pair.

**Token rotation:** The old refresh token is revoked immediately. Each refresh token can only be used once.

**Failure cases:**
- Token does not exist in the database
- Token has already been revoked (used)
- Token has expired (older than 7 days)

**Post-request script** automatically updates the collection variables with the new tokens.

header.description: JWT access token from login or refresh-token

request.description:
Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

Requires a valid JWT access token in the `Authorization: Bearer` header.

The `passwordHash` field is never included in the response. Includes `businessProfile` relation (null for personal users).

response.name: 200 - Personal User Profile
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890",
    "email": "mario.rossi@email.com",
    "firstName": "Mario",
    "lastName": "Rossi",
    "phone": "+393331234567",
    "codiceFiscale": null,
    "avatar": null,
    "role": "personal",
    "status": "active",
    "authProvider": "local",
    "firebaseUid": null,
    "emailVerified": true,
    "phoneVerified": false,
    "lastLoginAt": "2026-06-09T12:00:00.000Z",
    "createdAt": "2026-06-01T10:00:00.000Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-06-09T12:00:00.000Z",
    "businessProfile": null
  }
}
